comprehension_gate.instruction:
READ THIS ENTIRE PROMPT before posting your comprehension gate. Then READ all files listed in required_attachments (SDV §5.1 items 4-6, §5.3, §6, §7; the continuation XML ea_decomposition EA-2 block; docs/governance/STYLE.md verbatim; the three GOV tickets #18/#19/#20; and the primary anchor source files listed per-doc in SECTION 4 below). Then POST a comprehension gate comment on Vikunja Task 121, prefixed:
[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]
Your comprehension comment MUST contain ALL of the following sections (verbatim section headers required, in this exact order). Do NOT omit or merge headers.
A. MILESTONE OBJECTIVE (in your own words, 3-5 sentences — must name all three deliverables: gpu-runtime.md, error-recovery.md, circuit-breaker.md; must name the forward-reference to docs/governance/weight-integrity.md from error-recovery.md; must name STYLE.md as the binding style reference) B. WORK ITEMS (one sentence each for ALL work items WI-1 through WI-4 — do not summarize or group) C. FILES TO CREATE (list every new governance doc: gpu-runtime.md, error-recovery.md, circuit-breaker.md — plus the ledger entry line) D. FILES TO MODIFY (enumerate: only docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md) E. FILES TO READ (per-doc anchoring list — every source file cited in the per-WI anchor lists in SECTION 4 below, plus the ADRs named per-doc: ADR-011, ADR-012 §2.2 / §2.4 / §5, DEC-01..DEC-10) F. DELIVERABLE STRUCTURE (recite VERBATIM: branch name feature/p5-task9-ea2-runtime-resilience, every file path, the section structure from docs/governance/STYLE.md Doc Template, the ledger entry line, the expected commit message format). Recite STYLE.md's Doc Template headers VERBATIM: # {Domain Name} Governance ## Audience ## Prerequisites ## Source References ## Governance Content ## Recovery / Remediation Procedures ## Open Questions / Deferred Items G. ORACLE EXPECTATION (recite VERBATIM the two-step git diff --name-only commands and their expected empty output — both commands below MUST return zero lines): git diff main...feature/p5-task9-ea2-runtime-resilience --name-only | grep -vE "^docs/" git diff main...feature/p5-task9-ea2-runtime-resilience --name-only | grep -vE "^docs/governance/|^docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER\.md$|^docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN\.md$" H. STYLE.md CONFORMANCE ACKNOWLEDGMENT (quote L-18 verbatim; confirm you have READ docs/governance/STYLE.md from main HEAD and will conform to its Doc Template, Line-Count Floor, Source Anchoring, Audience Taxonomy, Markdown Conventions, Cross-Doc References, Filename Conventions, and Out-of-Scope-for-docs/governance/ sections) I. CROSS-SPRINT COEXISTENCE ACKNOWLEDGMENT (L-16): quote verbatim — "This sprint runs in parallel with Sprint 8. My writes are confined to docs/governance/. Sprint 8 writes are confined to **/tests/." J. PHANTOM-REFERENCE ACKNOWLEDGMENT (L-17): quote verbatim — "docs/governance/boot-sequence.md DOES NOT EXIST (phantom reference discovered during Sprint 9 planning; tracked in GOV-15 / Vikunja #124 for future authoring). If my doc would naturally cite a boot-sequence governance doc, I cite the relevant source files directly (e.g., services/*/src/boot.py, launcher/__main__.py) and note that boot-sequence governance is future-sprint scope." K. WEIGHT-INTEGRITY FORWARD-REFERENCE ACKNOWLEDGMENT: confirm that error-recovery.md (WI-2) will forward-reference docs/governance/weight-integrity.md as NOT-YET-AUTHORED (Pluton-blocked per GOV-10 / ISS-4) and that EA-2 will NOT author docs/governance/weight-integrity.md itself. L. SOURCE-ANCHORING RECITATION (per SDV §6 / success criterion 6): for each of the three governance docs, recite: - the GOV ticket ID + Vikunja task number - the ADR(s) it will cite (minimum 1 per doc) - at least one source file from the GOV ticket's "Scattered Sources" list (minimum 1 per doc) M. TARGET-AUDIENCE ASSIGNMENT (SDV §5.3, STYLE.md Audience Taxonomy): name the primary audience (operator / developer / auditor / incident responder / future agent) for each of the three governance docs. The "Audience" stanza opens each doc. N. 150-LINE FLOOR ACKNOWLEDGMENT (SDV §5.3 / §6 / STYLE.md Line-Count Floor): each of the three governance docs is ≥ 150 lines of substantive content. O. RISKS AND AMBIGUITIES (list any — at minimum: parallel-with-Sprint-8 context; whether source files cited below have shifted since GOV tickets were authored; whether the phantom-boot-sequence temptation materializes in gpu-runtime.md's drafting) P. PRODUCTION FILE PROHIBITION ACKNOWLEDGMENT (quote L-15 verbatim): "DO NOT modify any file outside docs/governance/ (except the ledger docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md at sprint-close entries and docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md at EA-5). If a non-governance file change appears necessary to unblock a doc (e.g., a stale CLAUDE.md claim contradicts a doc you're writing), STOP, document the blocker in your completion report, and wait for SDO direction. Do not make the change unilaterally."
STOP after posting the comprehension gate. Do NOT proceed to implementation. Wait for SDO [agent:sdo][phase:comprehension-review] VERDICT: APPROVED on Vikunja Task 121 before starting any work.

milestone.objective: Author three governance documents under docs/governance/ that consolidate BlarAI's runtime-behavior and resilience surface: gpu-runtime.md (GOV-05 — model-load, speculative decoding, KV-cache, thinking-mode suppression, empirical baselines, model rollback), error-recovery.md (GOV-06 — fail-closed per subsystem, error classes, retry vs escalation matrix, user-facing messages, audit trail), and circuit-breaker.md (GOV-07 — MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS and MAX_TOOL_CALL_DEPTH breakers, trip behavior, fallback text, reset semantics, PGOV+streaming interaction). Each doc conforms verbatim to docs/governance/ STYLE.md's Doc Template. Each doc anchors to the production source files enumerated in SECTION 4 below and to the ADRs locked for the runtime surface (ADR-011, ADR-012). This EA does NOT author docs/governance/weight-integrity.md — that GOV-10 doc is Pluton-blocked pending ISS-4; error-recovery.md forward-references it as a deferred artifact.
milestone.ledger_entry_reserved: EA Code MUST scan docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md at commit time for the highest existing ### Entry N and use N+1. The target entry follows Sprint 8 advances + Sprint 9 EA-1 — likely 53 or higher at EA-2 commit time. SDO does NOT hard-code a specific number here; the next-free discipline supersedes any projection.
milestone.sizing: M (three deliverables; follows STYLE.md precedent committed by EA-1)
milestone.depends_on: Sprint 9 EA-1 merged to main (commit b0f0bde — docs/governance/STYLE.md, pgov-validation.md, ipc-protocol.md, streaming-output.md exist on main). STYLE.md is the binding style reference for EA-2.
milestone.parallel_with: Sprint 8 (Task 82). Working sets disjoint per L-16: Sprint 8 = **/tests/; Sprint 9 = docs/governance/**.

work_item.title: Author docs/governance/gpu-runtime.md — GPU Runtime & Speculative Decoding Configuration Governance
work_item.anchor_sources: Required (must cite at least these plus the listed ADRs): - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/model_loader.py (Qwen3-14B + draft model loading; cite the load sequence and device binding) - ADR-011 (all LLM inference on GPU — NPU retired from P1 Core Loop) - ADR-012 (Qwen3-14B unified model; speculative decoding mandatory) - ADR-012 §2.2 (num_assistant_tokens=3 lock for speculative decoding) - ADR-012 §2.4 (thinking-mode strategy — PA /no_think, AO thinking allowed with strip) - ADR-012 §5 (Qwen2.5-1.5B retained for rollback) Recommended additional (cite where it strengthens source anchoring): - DEC-01..DEC-10 (Task 4 production configuration decisions — cite the DEC numbers most directly load-bearing for GPU runtime, e.g., memory headroom decisions, KV-cache sizing decisions) - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/circuit_breaker.py (KV-cache interaction with circuit-breaker trip) - phase2_gates/evidence/* (if P5-005b or equivalent empirical baseline evidence exists, cite by path)
work_item.required_coverage: The doc MUST cover EACH of the following (minimum bar from GOV-05 ticket + SDV §5.1 item 4): 1. Model-load procedure for Qwen3-14B INT4 on Arc 140V Xe2 GPU. 2. Draft-model load (Qwen3-0.6B INT4) and its device binding. 3. Speculative-decoding configuration: num_assistant_tokens=3 lock per ADR-012 §2.2; rationale for the specific value. 4. KV-cache management: FP16 retention; approximate sizing (PA ~350-550MB, AO ~1-1.2GB) — cite the source of these numbers. 5. Circuit-breaker trip effect on KV-cache: what happens to cached state when MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS trips mid-stream? (forward-reference circuit-breaker.md — which WI-3 will author). 6. Thinking-mode suppression mechanics: /no_think prefix for PA per ADR-012 §2.4 stop-token IDs [151645, 151668] for thinking-block truncation AO thinking allowed with strip at transport layer 7. XAttention OFF rationale (cite the decision record or the model_loader source lines that set the flag). 8. Empirical performance baseline (if available): 10.72 tps @ 4K context 4.17 tps @ 20K context cite the phase2_gates evidence path or the commit that recorded the measurement. If evidence is not locatable at authoring time, note the gap explicitly and cite the closest available reference. 9. Model rollback procedure: Qwen2.5-1.5B retained per ADR-012 §5; runbook steps to switch active model if Qwen3-14B fails validation. 10. Memory ceiling awareness: 31.323 GB effective (32 GB LPDDR5X minus 693 MB firmware reservation); how runtime stays under it. PHANTOM REFERENCE WARNING (L-17): If the GOV-05 ticket body or any source file mentions a boot-sequence governance doc (docs/governance/boot-sequence.md), DO NOT cite that path. Cite the underlying source file(s) instead (services/assistant_orchestrator/src/model_loader.py, launcher/__main__.py, services/policy_agent/src/* startup entry points) and note that boot-sequence governance is future-sprint scope tracked in GOV-15 / Vikunja #124.
work_item.negative_constraints: - Do NOT cite docs/governance/boot-sequence.md (L-17 phantom — see above). - Do NOT cite or modify docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md (deferred to GOV-MIGRATE / Vikunja #123). - Do NOT propose model-switch automation or NPU re-enablement; ADR-011 retires the NPU from P1 Core Loop — describe current GPU-only state. - Do NOT modify any file outside docs/governance/ (plus the ledger at WI-4). - Do NOT author circuit-breaker trip semantics inline beyond forward-reference; that is WI-3's territory.

work_item.title: Author docs/governance/error-recovery.md — Error Handling & Crash Recovery Governance
work_item.anchor_sources: Required (must cite at least these plus the listed ADRs): - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/error_handling.py (fail-closed error surface — cite the specific exception classes and their escalation paths) - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/model_loader.py (model-load failure handling) - ADR-011 (GPU-only inference — GPU-fault recovery posture) - ADR-012 §5 (Qwen2.5-1.5B rollback path; when to invoke) Recommended additional (cite where it strengthens source anchoring): - services/policy_agent/src/error_handling.py (if exists — PA adjudication error path) - shared/ipc/protocol.py (vsock IPC failure surface) - shared/schemas/car.py (CAR hash-verification failure path) - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/circuit_breaker.py (circuit-breaker as preventative-recovery surface — forward-reference WI-3)
work_item.required_coverage: The doc MUST cover EACH of the following (minimum bar from GOV-06 ticket + SDV §5.1 item 5): 1. Fail-Closed principle per subsystem: PA, AO, IPC, TUI, launcher. For each, cite the source file that enforces fail-closed behavior. 2. PA adjudication errors: what triggers; user-visible outcome; log trail. 3. AO generation errors: mid-stream crash; partial-output handling; PGOV interaction (does partial output get validated or discarded?). 4. JWT / mTLS failure handling: what the sender sees; what the receiver does; whether retries are permitted. 5. Model-load failure: Qwen3-14B fails to load → which recovery path? (ADR-012 §5 Qwen2.5-1.5B rollback? fail-closed system halt?) — cite the source file that decides. 6. Weight-integrity check failure: FORWARD-REFERENCE docs/governance/weight-integrity.md as NOT-YET-AUTHORED (Pluton-blocked per GOV-10 / ISS-4). DO NOT describe the Pluton ceremony here. Use a clear deferred marker: "Weight-integrity check procedure is governed by the future [weight-integrity.md](weight-integrity.md) doc (GOV-10 / Vikunja #23), currently blocked on ISS-4 Pluton investigation. When that doc lands, this section links to its recovery subsection." Note: linking to a not-yet-authored markdown file produces a broken relative link until GOV-10 lands. That is ACCEPTABLE here because the gap is tracked and the forward-reference is the governance connective tissue. 7. vsock IPC failures: connection reset, message truncation, nonce replay detection; cite shared/ipc/*. 8. OOM handling: 31.323 GB memory ceiling; what triggers OOM in PA vs AO; recovery (kill + restart; model unload + reload). 9. User-facing error messages per error class: exact text surfaced to the operator (cite source files that emit the text). 10. Logging and audit trail: where errors land; retention; how an incident responder queries them. 11. Automatic retry vs manual escalation matrix: a table enumerating error classes, their retry policy (retry count / backoff / none), and their escalation trigger (alert / halt / Vikunja ticket). 12. Boot-time vs runtime failure distinction: a failure during launcher startup vs during live inference — which is recoverable; which requires operator intervention. PHANTOM-REFERENCE WARNING (L-17): do NOT cite a boot-sequence governance doc here; cite launcher/__main__.py and services/policy_agent/src/* startup entry points instead.
work_item.negative_constraints: - Do NOT author docs/governance/weight-integrity.md. It is Pluton-blocked per GOV-10 / ISS-4. Forward-reference it only. - Do NOT cite docs/governance/boot-sequence.md (L-17 phantom). - Do NOT describe Pluton-sealed manifest procedures; that is GOV-10 scope. - Do NOT author circuit-breaker trip semantics inline beyond forward-reference; WI-3 owns that. - Do NOT propose production-code error-handling changes.

work_item.title: Author docs/governance/circuit-breaker.md — Circuit Breaker Governance
work_item.anchor_sources: Required (must cite at least these plus the listed ADR): - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/circuit_breaker.py (full implementation — cite MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, MAX_TOOL_CALL_DEPTH constants with their line numbers; cite the trip logic) - ADR-012 §2.4 (thinking-mode strategy — thinking tokens counted or not against MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS?) Recommended additional (cite where it strengthens source anchoring): - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py (PGOV interaction — when PGOV runs relative to circuit-breaker trip) - services/ui_shell/src/streaming.py (streaming interaction — how trip terminates the stream) - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/model_loader.py (KV-cache interaction — what happens to cache on trip)
work_item.required_coverage: The doc MUST cover EACH of the following (minimum bar from GOV-07 ticket + SDV §5.1 item 6): 1. Two independent breakers: MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS = 4096 MAX_TOOL_CALL_DEPTH = 5 Cite the constant-definition line in circuit_breaker.py for each. Document the unit and semantics of each (tokens vs nested calls). 2. Token-counter semantics: does the counter include thinking tokens? (Per ADR-012 §2.4 AO is thinking-allowed-with-strip — document the observed behavior and cite the code that decides.) 3. Tool-call depth counter semantics: what counts as a "tool call"? how is nesting measured? cite source. 4. Trip behavior: on trip, what happens? Output truncation (mid-token? at safe boundary?) Fallback message emitted (exact text — cite source) Stream terminated (document semantics relative to UI buffer) 5. Exact fallback message text (cite source line where it lives). 6. Per-session vs cross-session reset semantics: Does a new session reset the breakers? Does a restart reset them? Is there any persistence of trip state across sessions? Cite source for each answer. 7. Threshold-tuning governance: Evidence required before changing MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS or MAX_TOOL_CALL_DEPTH. Who approves a change (ADR amendment vs DEC vs in-code). Reference Task 7 audit practice if applicable. 8. Monitoring: Breaker-trip logging (log level, destination, retention). Operator query: how to ask "did a breaker trip this session?" 9. Interaction with PGOV: PGOV runs AFTER generation (cite pgov.py). If breaker trips mid-generation, does PGOV still run on the truncated output? What is the user-visible result? 10. Interaction with streaming: Streaming emits tokens incrementally (cite streaming.py). On trip, how is the StreamingDisplay signaled? Does the TUI show a trip indicator? 11. Interaction with KV-cache: Does the cache persist across a trip? Is it invalidated? Cite the model_loader or circuit_breaker source that decides. 12. Example scenarios (narrative): a 5000-token response that trips MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS; a 6-level tool-call chain that trips MAX_TOOL_CALL_DEPTH. For each, describe the user experience end-to-end.
work_item.negative_constraints: - Do NOT propose threshold changes; document current thresholds and the governance for changing them. - Do NOT author PGOV stage-level governance; that is pgov-validation.md (already on main). Forward-reference it. - Do NOT author streaming-lifecycle governance; that is streaming-output.md (already on main). Forward-reference it. - Do NOT cite docs/governance/boot-sequence.md (L-17 phantom).

work_item.title: Add Sprint 9 EA-2 entry to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md
work_item.required_content:
Append a new ledger section with header: ### Entry N — Task 9 / EA-2: Sprint 9 Governance Documentation — Runtime Behavior & Resilience
Where N = (highest existing ### Entry number in the ledger) + 1. EA Code verifies by scanning the ledger at commit time and using the next-free number. SDO does NOT hard-code a projection; Sprint 8 advances + Sprint 9 EA-1 have likely pushed N to 53 or higher.
Body MUST include: - Commit hash of the EA-2 branch HEAD (Co-Lead's merge commit supersedes if the PR is merge-committed rather than fast-forwarded). - Three deliverables authored with their file paths and approximate line counts (wc -l output). - STYLE.md-conformance confirmation (L-18 satisfied). - Source-anchoring summary: ADR cites per doc + anchor-source files per doc. - Parallel-with-Sprint-8 note (L-16 coexistence). - Phantom-reference handling note (L-17): gpu-runtime.md and error-recovery.md DID NOT cite docs/governance/boot-sequence.md; cited source files directly. - Weight-integrity deferral note: error-recovery.md forward-references docs/governance/weight-integrity.md; GOV-10 remains Pluton-blocked pending ISS-4. - Test-baseline confirmation: regression suite unchanged (baseline at SDO authoring time — 755 passed, 2 skipped REGRESSION; may have advanced post-Sprint-8 EA merges — EA Code cites the exact observed count).

deliverable.files_to_create: - docs/governance/gpu-runtime.md (≥ 150 lines; GOV-05 / Vikunja #18) - docs/governance/error-recovery.md (≥ 150 lines; GOV-06 / Vikunja #19) - docs/governance/circuit-breaker.md (≥ 150 lines; GOV-07 / Vikunja #20)
deliverable.section_structure_per_doc:
Every governance doc (WI-1, WI-2, WI-3) follows docs/governance/STYLE.md's Doc Template VERBATIM. The required section headers in order:
# {Domain Name} Governance ## Audience ## Prerequisites ## Source References ## Governance Content ## Recovery / Remediation Procedures ## Open Questions / Deferred Items
Per STYLE.md "Per-doc-type flex": the "## Recovery / Remediation Procedures" header MAY be omitted entirely (no stub) when a standalone recovery section would duplicate Governance Content or when the domain has no externally-triggered failure mode. For EA-2: - gpu-runtime.md: Recovery section REQUIRED (model rollback runbook is the recovery surface). - error-recovery.md: Recovery section REQUIRED AND IS THE DOC — the distinction between Governance Content and Recovery may be thin; use judgment and STYLE.md's flex rule. If merged, omit the separate Recovery header entirely; do not leave a stub. - circuit-breaker.md: Recovery section OPTIONAL — trip behavior IS the recovery (output truncated + fallback shown); if merging into Governance Content is clearer, omit the separate header.
The Audience stanza cites from: operator / developer / auditor / incident responder / future agent. Source References cite ADRs and at least one source file from the GOV ticket's Scattered Sources list. Governance Content is the substantive body. Open Questions / Deferred Items notes the weight-integrity.md deferral in error-recovery.md and any phantom boot-sequence gaps in gpu-runtime.md.

negative_constraints.constraint:
Verbatim from the Sprint 9 SDO continuation lessons-learned (adapted for Sprint 9 scope): "DO NOT modify any file outside docs/governance/ (except the ledger docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md at sprint-close entries and docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md at EA-5). If a non-governance file change appears necessary to unblock a doc (e.g., a stale CLAUDE.md claim contradicts a doc you're writing), STOP, document the blocker in your completion report, and wait for SDO direction. Do not make the change unilaterally."
Specifically PROHIBITED file paths for Sprint 9 EA-2: - **/tests/* (Sprint 8 territory) - shared/** (production code) - services/*/src/** (production code) - launcher/** (production code) - pyproject.toml - conftest.py (anywhere in the tree) - docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md (deferred to GOV-MIGRATE / Vikunja #123) - Any ADR file (docs reference ADRs; they do NOT modify them) - docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md (reserved for EA-5 at sprint close — NOT for EA-2) If the diff shows any file in the above list, the ORACLE gate fails and the EA MUST halt and escalate to SDO via Task 121 comment before attempting fixes.

Verbatim: "DO NOT write under **/tests/, shared/, services/*/src/, launcher/, or any path owned by Sprint 8." Cross-sprint boundary: Sprint 9 writes ONLY docs/governance/** (and the ledger). If during authoring you discover a test that would benefit from addition, or a production-code defect, do NOT add/fix it — that is Sprint 8's or a later sprint's territory. Document the observation in WI-4 ledger entry and move on.

Verbatim from the Sprint 9 SDO continuation: "docs/governance/boot-sequence.md DOES NOT EXIST (phantom reference discovered during Sprint 9 planning; tracked in GOV-15 / Vikunja #124 for future authoring). If your doc would naturally cite a boot-sequence governance doc, cite the relevant source files directly (e.g., services/*/src/boot.py, launcher/__main__.py) and note that boot-sequence governance is future-sprint scope."
This constraint applies with particular force to WI-1 (gpu-runtime.md) and WI-2 (error-recovery.md §12 boot-time vs runtime failure). Do NOT backfill by creating docs/governance/boot-sequence.md. Cite source files.

STYLE.md already exists on main at docs/governance/STYLE.md (committed by Sprint 9 EA-1 at b0f0bde). Read it BEFORE authoring any WI. Conform to its Doc Template, section ordering, Line-Count Floor, Source Anchoring, Audience Taxonomy, Markdown Conventions, Cross-Doc References, Filename Conventions, and Out-of-Scope-for-docs/governance/ sections verbatim. Do NOT amend or modify STYLE.md in this EA.

Pluton-blocked content is out of scope: - GOV-01 Credentials & Certificate Lifecycle — do NOT author docs/governance/credentials-lifecycle.md in EA-2. - GOV-10 Weight Integrity Verification — do NOT author docs/governance/weight-integrity.md in EA-2. error-recovery.md FORWARD-REFERENCES it only; the file is NOT authored in this sprint. These remain blocked on ISS-4 Pluton investigation.

Governance docs reference ADRs; they do NOT modify ADR content. If a doc surfaces an ADR ambiguity during authoring, document it in the WI-4 ledger entry and open a new Vikunja ticket. Do NOT edit any ADR file.
docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md is the legacy test-policy doc; migration into docs/governance/ is deferred to GOV-MIGRATE / Vikunja #123 after Sprint 8 closes. Do NOT modify, move, or delete it in EA-2.
Do NOT author or modify any doc-lint tool, governance-portal web view, or markdown-rendering automation. EA-2 authors three prose markdown docs; tooling is out of scope.

Sprint 9 EA work MUST NOT reference Sprint 8 branches, test files, or commit hashes inside the governance docs. The governance docs are timeless references to production code state, not sprint-execution logs. The ledger entry (WI-4) MAY mention the parallel-with-Sprint-8 context because the ledger IS a sprint-execution log.

quality_gate.gate: Every authored markdown file passes basic structural lint: - exactly one H1 (# ...) per doc - H2 section ordering matches docs/governance/STYLE.md's Doc Template (with per-doc-type flex for Recovery section per STYLE.md) - all fenced code blocks are closed - no broken relative links EXCEPT the sanctioned forward-reference from error-recovery.md to weight-integrity.md (which is a Pluton-blocked deferred file and intentionally produces a broken link until GOV-10 lands) Command: eyeball each doc + `grep -E "^# " docs/governance/*.md` to confirm exactly one H1 per file.

Each of WI-1, WI-2, WI-3 cites: - at least 1 ADR (by ADR number — ADR-011 or ADR-012) - at least 1 source file from its GOV ticket's Scattered Sources list Commands: grep -E "ADR-(011|012)" docs/governance/gpu-runtime.md grep -E "services/|shared/|launcher/" docs/governance/gpu-runtime.md grep -E "ADR-(011|012)" docs/governance/error-recovery.md grep -E "services/|shared/|launcher/" docs/governance/error-recovery.md grep -E "ADR-(011|012)" docs/governance/circuit-breaker.md grep -E "services/|shared/|launcher/" docs/governance/circuit-breaker.md Each command must return ≥ 1 match.
Each of WI-1, WI-2, WI-3 is ≥ 150 lines. Command: wc -l docs/governance/gpu-runtime.md docs/governance/error-recovery.md docs/governance/circuit-breaker.md Expected: each ≥ 150.

TWO-STEP gate — BOTH commands MUST produce empty output:
Step 1 (docs-only): git diff main...feature/p5-task9-ea2-runtime-resilience --name-only | grep -vE "^docs/" Expected output: EMPTY (zero lines).
Step 2 (governance-scoped within docs): git diff main...feature/p5-task9-ea2-runtime-resilience --name-only | grep -vE "^docs/governance/|^docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER\.md$|^docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN\.md$" Expected output: EMPTY (zero lines).
If ANY non-docs file or non-governance docs file appears in either output, the EA has violated Sprint 9's mandate. STOP, post completion with the ORACLE failure, and await SDO guidance. Do NOT attempt auto-fixes.
NOTE: docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md is permitted in Step 2 to keep the filter symmetric with EA-5's scope, but EA-2 MUST NOT actually modify docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md — that file is reserved for EA-5 at sprint close. If EA-2's diff includes docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, that is a scope violation and must be reverted before commit.

Sprint 9 does not touch tests, but we confirm the regression baseline is unaffected by Sprint 9 commits: .venv/Scripts/pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q Expected: ≥ 755 passed, 2 skipped (REGRESSION baseline at SDO authoring time). Sprint 8's in-flight EAs may have advanced the baseline; report the observed count verbatim in the completion comment.

verification.instruction: After all WIs are committed and the five quality gates pass, post a completion comment on Vikunja Task 121 prefixed [agent:ea_code][phase:completion] with: - commit hash of the latest commit on the EA-2 branch - output of: git log --oneline main..HEAD - output of: git diff main...HEAD --name-only - output of: wc -l docs/governance/gpu-runtime.md docs/governance/error-recovery.md docs/governance/circuit-breaker.md - output of: pytest run summary (first/last lines) - a per-doc one-sentence synopsis + anchor-source confirmation per doc - ORACLE gate result for BOTH Step 1 and Step 2 (PASS / FAIL; if FAIL, do NOT proceed to merge — escalate to SDO) - Phantom-reference handling confirmation (no boot-sequence.md citations) - Weight-integrity forward-reference confirmation Apply Vikunja label: Gate:Pending-SDO (SDO will review on next cadence).
verification.terminal_commands: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git log --oneline -1 git diff main --name-only .venv/Scripts/pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q wc -l docs/governance/*.md
verification.expected_outputs:
git diff main --name-only (on the EA-2 branch vs main): docs/governance/circuit-breaker.md docs/governance/error-recovery.md docs/governance/gpu-runtime.md docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md
wc -l (on the three new docs): ≥ 150 lines each for gpu-runtime.md, error-recovery.md, circuit-breaker.md. STYLE.md (already committed) and EA-1's three docs are unchanged.
pytest summary: ≥ 755 passed, 2 skipped (or higher if Sprint 8 EAs have merged; cite exact observed counts).

ea_prompt.commit_template:
Final EA commit (after all WIs are authored and gates pass):
docs(task9/ea2): runtime + error-recovery + circuit-breaker governance — 3 new docs, {L} lines total
- docs/governance/gpu-runtime.md (GOV-05, HIGH — runtime surface) - docs/governance/error-recovery.md (GOV-06, HIGH — resilience surface) - docs/governance/circuit-breaker.md (GOV-07, HIGH — runaway containment) - docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (Entry N — Task 9 / EA-2)
Sprint 9 parallel with Sprint 8 per L-16; ORACLE two-step gate PASS. ADR cites: ADR-011 (GPU inference), ADR-012 §2.2/§2.4/§5 (Qwen3-14B + speculative decoding + thinking mode + rollback), DEC-01..DEC-10. Anchor sources: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/model_loader.py, services/assistant_orchestrator/src/error_handling.py, services/assistant_orchestrator/src/circuit_breaker.py.
STYLE.md conformance verified. Phantom boot-sequence.md NOT cited (L-17 respected). Weight-integrity.md forward-referenced as GOV-10 Pluton-blocked deferred artifact.
Ledger entry added: Entry N, Sprint 9 EA-2 Runtime Behavior & Resilience.
EA Code fills {L} at commit time as the sum of wc -l across the three new docs.

mature_not_minimal.line_floor:
Each of gpu-runtime.md, error-recovery.md, circuit-breaker.md: ≥ 150 lines of SUBSTANTIVE content. Short docs are a signal that the required-coverage list has not been exhaustively addressed. If a doc comes in under 150 lines with ALL required_coverage items addressed, the EA MUST explicitly justify the deficit in the completion comment; this is a reviewable position, not an automatic failure.
GOV-05 (gpu-runtime.md) is flagged in SDV §9.2 as likely 400+ lines because speculative decoding + KV-cache + thinking-mode suppression + empirical baseline + rollback all live together. Do not under-invest.
mature_not_minimal.cross_referencing:
Adjacent cross-referencing is permitted and encouraged. Specifically: - gpu-runtime.md MAY forward-reference circuit-breaker.md (WI-3) for KV-cache-on-trip semantics. - error-recovery.md MAY forward-reference circuit-breaker.md for preventative-recovery framing. - All three MAY reference the already-committed EA-1 docs: pgov-validation.md (e.g., PGOV error as fail-closed example in error-recovery.md), ipc-protocol.md (e.g., vsock failure as IPC error class), streaming-output.md (e.g., streaming termination on circuit-breaker trip). The EA MUST NOT retroactively edit already-committed docs in this EA unless the edit is a pure link-text correction (no content shift). If a genuine content gap emerges in an already-committed doc, document it in WI-4 ledger entry and escalate to SDO for a follow-up EA patch.
Retroactive-edit prohibition explicitly covers EA-1's committed docs: pgov-validation.md, ipc-protocol.md, streaming-output.md, STYLE.md. If the EA's drafting reveals a gap in any of these, raise it in WI-4 and STOP — do NOT edit them inline.
mature_not_minimal.source_anchoring: Every governance doc cites ≥ 1 ADR and ≥ 1 source file with a repo-relative path. Where practical, cite line ranges (e.g., services/assistant_orchestrator/src/circuit_breaker.py lines 12-38). Line-range citations age; path + section/constant name (e.g., "circuit_breaker.py MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS") is more durable where the structure supports it — prefer that form per STYLE.md Source Anchoring.

required_attachments.attachment: docs/P5_TASK9_SDO_CONTINUATION_v1.0.xml (full — read ea_decomposition EA-2 block §5 and lessons L-12/L-13/L-15/L-16/L-17/L-18 §6 verbatim)
docs/sprints/sprint_9/strategic_design_vision.md (§§4, 5.1 items 4-6, 5.2, 5.3, 6, 7, 9, 11)
docs/governance/STYLE.md (L-18 CRITICAL — binding style reference committed by EA-1 at b0f0bde; read verbatim and conform)
docs/governance/pgov-validation.md (EA-1 peer doc — for cross-referencing and style precedent)
docs/governance/ipc-protocol.md (EA-1 peer doc — for cross-referencing and style precedent)
docs/governance/streaming-output.md (EA-1 peer doc — for cross-referencing and style precedent)
Vikunja Task 18 (GOV-05 GPU Runtime)
Vikunja Task 19 (GOV-06 Error Recovery)
Vikunja Task 20 (GOV-07 Circuit Breaker)
services/assistant_orchestrator/src/model_loader.py (WI-1 and WI-2 anchor — Qwen3-14B + draft loading + model-load failure path)
services/assistant_orchestrator/src/circuit_breaker.py (WI-3 primary anchor — MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, MAX_TOOL_CALL_DEPTH)
services/assistant_orchestrator/src/error_handling.py (WI-2 primary anchor — fail-closed surface)
services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py (WI-3 anchor — PGOV+circuit-breaker interaction)
services/ui_shell/src/streaming.py (WI-3 anchor — streaming+trip interaction)
shared/ipc/protocol.py (WI-2 anchor — vsock IPC failure surface; read if exists)
shared/schemas/car.py (WI-2 anchor — CAR hash-verification failure)
launcher/__main__.py (WI-1 and WI-2 anchor — boot-time entry; replaces phantom boot-sequence.md citations)
docs/adrs/ (or wherever ADRs live — EA locates ADR-011 and ADR-012)
docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (for WI-4 — scan for highest ### Entry N)
CLAUDE.md (for DEC-01..DEC-10 references and the locked architectural decisions table — for WI-1 gpu-runtime.md DEC citations)
phase2_gates/evidence/ (if exists; P5-005b or equivalent empirical baseline evidence for WI-1 performance baseline section)
